nameMeanings.name: Whyte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (central Scotland): variant of   White  . This form of the surname is common mainly in Ireland and Scotland.

variants.name: Whyde
meanings: English: perhaps an altered form of   Whyte   or   Hyde  .

variants.name: White
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Whyte was born about 1500, in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland as the daughter of Mr Whyte and Mrs Whyte. She had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with John Drysdaill.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Whyte est née environ 1500, à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, fille de Mr Whyte et Mrs Whyte. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec John Drysdaill.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Whyte wurde um 1500 in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, als Tochter von Mr Whyte und Mrs Whyte geboren. Sie hatte mit John Drysdaill mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Whyte è nata il circa 1500 nel Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland come figlia di Mr Whyte e Mrs Whyte. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con John Drysdaill.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Whyte(은)는 대략 1500년 스컷랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에서 Mr Whyte(와)과 Mrs Whyte의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 John Drysdaill(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.
